gleam 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Compute Daily Enteric Methane Emissions

Source: R/directemissions_enteric_core_model.R
compute_daily_enteric_emissions.Rd

Calculates daily enteric methane emissions (kg CH₄/head/day) based on gross energy intake, methane conversion factor (ym), and dry matter intake (dmi).

compute_daily_enteric_emissions(
  animal,
  ym,
  ch4_mitigation_factor,
  diet_ge,
  dmi
)

Arguments

animal

Character. Code identifying the livestock species. Supported values include:

  • PGS: pigs

  • CML: camels

  • CTL: cattle

  • BFL: buffalo

  • SHP: sheep

  • GTS: goats

ym

Numeric. Methane conversion factor (ym), representing the percentage of gross energy of the feed ration that is converted to CH₄ (percentage).

ch4_mitigation_factor

Numeric. Dimensionless fraction of baseline enteric methane emissions remaining after mitigation. Applied as a multiplicative factor to calculated emissions (1 = no mitigation, 0.9 = 10% reduction). Default = 1.

diet_ge

Numeric. Average gross energy content of the diet (MJ/kg DM).

dmi

Numeric. Average daily dry matter intake of feed (kg dry matter/head/day).

Value

Numeric. Average daily enteric methane emissions (kg CH₄/head/day).

Details

The formula used to estimate daily enteric methane emissions is:

$$CH_4 = \frac{diet\_ge \times dmi \times \frac{ym}{100}}{55.65}$$

where 55.65 MJ/kg is the energy content of methane.

The function returns NA for chickens.

References

IPCC. (2019). 2019 Refinement to the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Chapter 10: Emissions from Livestock and Manure Management, Equation 10.21.

IPCC. (2006). 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Chapter 10: Emissions from Livestock and Manure Management, Equation 10.21.

Contents

Developed by Ahmed Jou, Yassine Elaouni, Dominik Wisser, Lydia Lanzoni, Giuseppe Tempio, Federal Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Regional Identity of Germany BMELH.
